title: Van der Weyden's The Descent from the Cross
description: This quiz is for the video Rogier van der Weyden, The Descent from the Cross.
topic_path: Van der Weyden's The Descent from the Cross

assessment_items.question_content:
**This scene of the *The Descent from the Cross* shows Christ being removed from the cross and is one of the standard scenes from the life of Christ.**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a962d3f2973262a709326d68eae767a4.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **This is correct. This scene of the Deposition shows Christ being removed from the cross and is one of the standard scenes from the life of Christ.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Van der Weyden's *The Descent from the Cross* reveals many artistic conventions of the Northern Renaissance. Which of the following is NOT present in the painting?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a962d3f2973262a709326d68eae767a4.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Van der Weyden did not choose to include a landscape in the *Deposition*, though many Northern Renaissance do include detailed landscapes.**

choices.content: An interest in light and texture

assessment_items.question_content:
**Van der Weyden gives his figures great emotional intensity combined with great attention to detail. These allow the viewer . . .**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a962d3f2973262a709326d68eae767a4.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The artist gives his figures great emotional intensity combined with great attention to detail. These allow the viewer to empathize with the figures in the scene.**

choices.content: to judge and critique the figures in the scene.
to feel at a great distance from the scene.
to empathize with the figures in the scene.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Van der Weyden carefully constructed the composition of his *The Descent from the Cross*. Which of the following does NOT describe the composition of the painting?**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a962d3f2973262a709326d68eae767a4.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Van der Weyden uses a flexible symmetry to organize the secondary figures in the scene.**

choices.content: Van der Weyden uses the same pose for Mary and for Christ, anticipating the Lamentation, when Christ's dead body will be placed in her lap.
Van der Weyden balances his composition fully filling less-occupied spaces with detail and texture.
The figures curl inward to focus the viewer's attention on Christ.
Van der Weyden uses rigid symmetry to organize the figures in the painting.
